changelog: Initial release: plugin loading, sidebar list, custom window design, settings persistence, auto-update
Add: applying downloaded updates (Updater.exe backs up the previous version, replaces host files, restarts the host)
Add: download/extract progress and error/retry states in the update overlay
Change: Updater.exe ships inside each release zip instead of the install folder, and deletes itself after applying an update
Fix: test release for verifying the update-installation flow end to end
Fix: an unhandled exception if Updater.exe failed to start (e.g. blocked by antivirus) silently crashed the host instead of showing an error
Add: show the reason in the title bar when the update check itself fails (e.g. GitHub API rate limit), instead of silently showing nothing
Fix: Updater.exe could fail silently while copying files (e.g. a file briefly locked after the host closed), leaving neither the old nor new version running
Add: Updater.exe now retries locked-file errors, restores the previous version from backup if the update itself fails, and writes progress to Release/Updater.log
Fix: a race between the host closing itself and Updater.exe starting up could leave neither the old nor new host running
Change: the host no longer closes itself after launching Updater.exe — Updater.exe now kills the host process by PID before replacing files

Fix: Updater.exe never received its arguments correctly because the install path ends in a backslash, which escapes the closing quote in the Windows command line and corrupts argument parsing — clicking Update would get stuck on "Installing update..." forever
Change: pass arguments to Updater.exe via ArgumentList instead of a manually-quoted string
Add: application icon ("Sn" on accent background) in the title bar, taskbar, and .exe
Add: custom application-wide font (Fontin Bold)
Add: live CPU/RAM usage of the SnHelper process on the Performance tab
Add: check for updates every 10 minutes while the app is running, not just at startup
Change: the released host is now a single SnHelper.exe (framework-dependent, .NET Desktop Runtime still required on the user's machine) instead of an exe with separate .dll/.runtimeconfig.json files
Add: landing page on GitHub Pages (SnHelper-releases), showing the current version and a download link, updated automatically on each publish
Change: update checks now read version.json on GitHub Pages instead of the GitHub Releases API, so they're no longer subject to the 60-requests-per-hour anonymous rate limit
Change: periodic update check interval reduced from 10 minutes to 20 seconds, now that it's no longer rate-limited
Add: update overlay now shows the changelog of every version between the currently installed one and the latest, not just the latest
